content.text:
{
  "dimension": "intervention-quality",
  "score": 3,
  "rationale": "The coach's moves are precisely matched to live reads and deployed at moments the client can receive them. Turn 2 names the intellectual/emotional mismatch and pivots rather than answering the surface question. Turn 4 uses the 'most profound positive shift' question to find the real want, which lands immediately (turn 5). Turns 10-14 walk the client into the awareness-vs-parts distinction by question, not assertion. Turn 16 dissolves identification with parts work without devaluing it by offering alternative framings. Turn 22 is the signature inversion 'how do you not trust it?' deployed at the exact moment the client locks into a 'how do I do X' frame. Turns 24-32 use the present-moment evidence ('you just spoke') to demonstrate the answer rather than explain it — and the coach holds the moment, repeating and slowing rather than rushing to insight. Turn 38 delivers the principle reframe ('if you think you're making a decision, you're in fear') only after the experiential ground has been laid, then grounds it with concrete micro-examples in turn 40. Sequence is clean: regulation/presence → real want → dissolve framing → present-moment proof → principle.",
  "evidence_turns": [2, 4, 10, 12, 16, 22, 26, 30, 32, 34, 38, 40],
  "turn_annotations": [
    {"turn": 2, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Names the read (intellectual question, emotional body elsewhere) and offers a pivot rather than answering — perfectly matched move."},
    {"turn": 4, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "'Most profound positive shift' question to find the real want behind the rehearsed topic. Lands."},
    {"turn": 10, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "'What's you?' — sharp inversion that opens the awareness-vs-parts distinction."},
    {"turn": 12, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Stays with it when client gives the easy answer; presses with a concrete test."},
    {"turn": 16, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Dissolves attachment to parts-work framework by offering alternatives — without devaluing it. Right move at right time."},
    {"turn": 18, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Names the insight precisely: thoughts are both false and true."},
    {"turn": 22, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Signature inversion deployed at the exact moment the client frames the issue as 'how do I trust X.'"},
    {"turn": 26, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "'You just spoke.' Points to present-moment evidence. Holds the moment rather than explaining."},
    {"turn": 30, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "'It's happening right now.' Coach repeats and slows; lets the move land instead of moving on."},
    {"turn": 32, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Synthesis weaves parts + awareness + present-moment evidence into one move."},
    {"turn": 34, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Resolves the original question by showing it's already happening — dissolution as answer."},
    {"turn": 38, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Principle reframe arrives only after experiential ground is laid."},
    {"turn": 40, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Grounds abstract principle with concrete micro-decisions just witnessed."}
  ],
  "expected_territory_hits": ["follow-wonder-flag-the-deflection", "i-dont-buy-your-story", "find-the-want-behind-the-should", "how-do-you-not-trust-it", "whats-the-most-profound-positive-shift", "what-am-i", "idea-of-self-vs-actual-self", "view"],
  "expected_territory_misses": ["press-the-button-thought-experiment", "anxiety-three-definitions", "emotions-and-stories-dance"],
  "notes": "The 'misses' aren't actual misses — they're alternative tools that weren't needed because the chosen path worked. Per the trap note: dissolving the framing IS the answer here."
}
